hypothesis: AMDEN, a diffusion model with an EGNN backbone (VE for positions and VP for element embeddings) and optional energy-based score refinement with HMC, can generate amorphous materials whose structural and energetic properties (radial distribution functions, bond-angle distributions, structure factors, and potential energy distributions, before and after local optimization) closely match those of MD-generated reference datasets across different thermal histories, and incorporating energy-based HMC refinement and classifier-free guidance conditioning on Young's modulus further improves structural fidelity and enables targeted inverse design.
